name: Cross-border Data Policies
description: Policies that affect the ability to transfer data across borders, including restrictions, localization requirements, and international agreements.
scoring_note: Score 0 = low compliance cost (open). Score 1 = high compliance cost (restrictive). Score > 0 when: differential treatment of foreign providers, additional compliance costs online vs offline, or absence of key international norms.

indicators.name: Ban & local processing requirements
indicators.description: Outright bans on cross-border data transfers or requirements that data must be processed domestically.

scoring_guide.0.0: No ban or local processing requirement exists.
scoring_guide.0.5: Partial or conditional ban — transfers allowed under specific conditions or with authorization.
scoring_guide.1.0: Full ban on cross-border transfers or mandatory local processing with no exception pathway.

indicators.name: Local storage requirements
indicators.description: Requirements to store a copy of data on servers physically located within the country.

scoring_guide.0.5: Mirroring requirement — data may be transferred but a local copy must be retained.
scoring_guide.1.0: Strict local storage — all primary storage must be within national territory.

indicators.name: Infrastructure requirements
indicators.description: Requirements to maintain physical IT infrastructure (servers, data centres) within the country as a condition of operation.

scoring_guide.0.5: Infrastructure requirement applies only to certain sectors or data types.
scoring_guide.1.0: Broad infrastructure requirement applies to all digital service providers.

indicators.name: Conditional flow regimes
indicators.description: Transfer is permitted but subject to conditions such as adequacy decisions, standard contractual clauses, consent, or government approval.

scoring_guide.0.0: Transfers are free with no conditions.
scoring_guide.0.5: Transfers permitted under standard mechanisms (consent, contracts, adequacy) — moderate compliance cost.
scoring_guide.1.0: Transfers require case-by-case government approval — high compliance cost.

indicators.name: Not in an agreement with binding commitments on data transfer
indicators.description: Absence of participation in international agreements that provide binding commitments on cross-border data flows (e.g. CPTPP, DEPA, APEC CBPR).

scoring_guide.0.0: Country is party to one or more binding international data flow agreements.
scoring_guide.0.5: Country has non-binding commitments or is in negotiation.
scoring_guide.1.0: Country has no international binding commitments on data transfers.
